inputs.label: Observed Time (seconds)
inputs.businessContext: Average cycle time recorded during direct observation, excluding any abnormal delays.

inputs.label: Performance Rating (%)
inputs.businessContext: Operator pace relative to normal (100% = standard pace). Based on Westinghouse or similar rating system.

inputs.label: Allowance Percentage (%)
inputs.businessContext: Total allowance for fatigue, personal needs, and unavoidable delays (ISO 9001 / ILO guidelines).

inputs.label: Frequency per Cycle
inputs.businessContext: How many times the observed element occurs per complete work cycle (e.g., 2 inspections per assembly).

inputs.label: Work Measurement Method
inputs.businessContext: The technique used to obtain the observed time; affects confidence and adjustment factors.

inputs.label: Include Learning Curve Adjustment
inputs.businessContext: If true, applies a learning curve factor (80% slope) for new or changed processes.

rules.message: Observed time must be greater than 0 seconds.
Performance rating must be between 50% and 150%.
Allowance percentage must be between 0% and 50%.
Frequency per cycle must be positive.
Learning curve adjustment is not recommended with Work Sampling method due to high variability.

thresholds.message: Observed time exceeds typical single-element threshold; consider breaking down the element.
High performance rating may indicate rating bias; verify with video analysis.
Allowance above 20% may indicate excessive non-value-added time; review work conditions.

formulas.name: Normal Time
formulas.description: Observed time adjusted by performance rating.

formulas.name: Allowance Factor
formulas.description: Factor to convert normal time to standard time including allowances.

formulas.name: Standard Time per Occurrence
formulas.description: Normal time multiplied by allowance factor.

formulas.name: Standard Time per Cycle
formulas.description: Standard time per occurrence multiplied by frequency per cycle.

formulas.name: Learning Curve Factor
formulas.description: If learning curve is enabled, applies an 80% learning curve for the first 100 units (simplified).

formulas.name: Adjusted Standard Time
formulas.description: Standard time per cycle adjusted for learning curve.

formulas.name: Data Confidence Adjustment
formulas.description: Adjusts standard time based on measurement method reliability (ISO 9001:2015 clause 7.1.5).
formulas.formula: CASE work_measurement_method WHEN 'Stopwatch' THEN 1.05 WHEN 'Predetermined Motion Time' THEN 1.02 WHEN 'Work Sampling' THEN 1.10 WHEN 'Standard Data' THEN 1.03 ELSE 1.05 END

formulas.name: Standard Time (Primary Result)
formulas.description: Final standard time for the work element, adjusted for confidence.

primary.label: Standard Time
primary.description: The final standard time for the work element, incorporating all adjustments.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Excessive Allowance
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Allowance above 20% may indicate hidden losses due to poor workplace layout or ergonomic issues.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Low Performance Rating
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Rating below 80% suggests operator training gaps or process instability.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: High Frequency with Low Observed Time
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Frequent short elements may be prone to measurement error and cumulative fatigue.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Work Sampling Uncertainty
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Work Sampling has inherent statistical uncertainty; consider increasing sample size.

suggestedActions.action: Conduct ergonomic assessment to reduce physical strain and lower allowance.
Implement standardized work instructions and operator training program.
Decompose the work element into smaller sub-elements for more accurate measurement.
Increase number of observations to reduce confidence interval width.
Consider using Predetermined Motion Time Systems (e.g., MTM, MOST) to reduce rating bias.
